expectations.text: validar_solapamiento_contratos is identified as NOT needing optimization due to small n (3-8 contracts per propiedad)
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'With n ≤ 8, the O(n²) pairwise comparison performs at most 28 comparisons. This is trivially fast.' Code is preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: generar_rol_cobros is identified as already efficient (O(n) with HashMap pre-indexing) and left unchanged
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'Already applies the Space-Time Tradeoff paradigm correctly... Total complexity: O(n + m). This is optimal for this problem.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: propiedades_completamente_ocupadas is identified as already optimal and left unchanged
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'Already optimal for this dataset size — pre-indexing would add complexity without benefit.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: contratos_por_vencer is identified as already efficient O(n) and the BTreeMap suggestion is rejected as over-engineering
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'Optimal for this dataset size and call frequency. A BTreeMap index would be over-engineering.' Explicitly rejects the BTreeMap suggestion with reasoning about O(n log n) build cost vs single daily query.

expectations.text: ingreso_mensual_por_moneda is identified as already optimal and left unchanged
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'O(n) single pass with HashMap accumulation. Already optimal — textbook aggregation pattern.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: The output does NOT blindly optimize every function — at least 3 functions are explicitly left unchanged with justification
expectations.evidence: All 5 functions are explicitly left unchanged with individual justifications. Report concludes: 'No code changes are warranted. The output file is identical to the input.'

expectations.text: reporte_envejecimiento's BTreeMap is preserved (not replaced with HashMap) because sorted output order is required
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'The BTreeMap holds only 4 entries (the aging buckets) and ensures they appear in sorted order... Replacing BTreeMap with HashMap would break the sorted output requirement.' Code preserves BTreeMap.

expectations.text: inquilinos_sin_atrasos is refactored to use HashMap/HashSet lookups eliminating the triple nested loop
expectations.evidence: Code builds HashSet<Uuid> of contratos_con_atraso from pagos, then HashMap<Uuid, Vec<Uuid>> of contratos_por_inquilino. Report confirms: 'O(n × m × p) → O(n + m + p) using Space-Time Tradeoff.'

expectations.text: tendencia_pagos_acumulados is fixed with a cumulative/running total approach (not just HashMap grouping) to handle the prefix sum correctly
expectations.evidence: Code uses BTreeMap for grouping PLUS a running cumulative sum: 'let mut acumulado = 0.0; totales_por_mes.into_iter().map(|(mes, total_mes)| { acumulado += total_mes; (mes, acumulado) })'. Report identifies this as 'Transform and Conquer + Space-Time Tradeoff' with running total.

expectations.text: detectar_pagos_duplicados is refactored to use a composite key (contrato_id + fecha_vencimiento + monto) for grouping instead of O(n²) pairwise comparison
expectations.evidence: Code uses: 'let key = (pago.contrato_id, pago.fecha_vencimiento, pago.monto.to_bits())' as HashMap key. Report confirms: 'Group payments by composite key... Only payments in the same bucket can be duplicates.'

expectations.text: resumen_metodos_pago is identified as already efficient and its BTreeMap is preserved for chronological ordering
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'Already optimal. Single pass over payments, O(1) amortized insertion into nested maps. The outer BTreeMap ensures months appear in chronological order for the stacked bar chart.' Code preserves BTreeMap.

expectations.text: The output explains WHY each BTreeMap is kept or replaced, not just mechanically swapping all BTreeMaps for HashMaps
expectations.evidence: Report has a dedicated 'Key Decisions Explained' section: 'Why BTreeMap was kept in functions 1 and 5 — Both functions produce output for UI rendering that requires chronological/sorted ordering.' Also explains why tendencia uses BTreeMap instead of sort+dedup.

expectations.text: aplicar_pago_parcial is identified as NOT needing optimization (n=1-12 is too small, sort is appropriate for the greedy allocation)
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'With n = 1-12 elements, the sort is essentially free. The greedy approach is provably correct... The min-heap suggestion is a false optimization — it doesn't improve complexity and hurts readability for tiny n.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: rentabilidad_por_propiedad_mes is refactored with multi-level pre-indexing (contrato-to-propiedad mapping AND pagos grouped by contrato AND gastos grouped by propiedad+month)
expectations.evidence: Code builds: (1) contrato_to_propiedad HashMap, (2) ingresos_map keyed by (propiedad_id, month), (3) egresos_map keyed by (propiedad_id, month). Report confirms: 'Build three lookup structures in single passes... Then assembly is O(P × M) with O(1) lookups.'

expectations.text: mejor_mes_mantenimiento is identified as already O(n) (12 is a constant) and left unchanged or noted as not needing optimization
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'The outer loop runs exactly 12 times (fixed constant). The inner loop scans ~200 contracts. Total work: 12 × 200 = 2,400 comparisons. This is O(n).' Explicitly rejects interval tree and sweep line. Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: cascada_morosidad is fixed using a prefix sum or running total approach (group by month first, then accumulate) rather than just HashMap grouping
expectations.evidence: Code groups unpaid counts per month, then computes prefix sum: 'let mut acumulado: usize = 0; meses.iter().map(|mes| { let resultado = (mes.clone(), acumulado); acumulado += impagos_por_mes.get(mes)...; resultado })'. Report identifies this as 'Dynamic Programming (prefix sum / cumulative computation)'.

expectations.text: The output correctly rejects at least one 'clever' suggestion (min-heap, interval tree) as over-engineering with clear reasoning about why
expectations.evidence: Report rejects BOTH: (1) min-heap for aplicar_pago_parcial: 'heap extraction is O(n log n) total, same as sort... sort produces a contiguous sorted slice — better cache locality', (2) interval tree for mejor_mes_mantenimiento: 'construction cost O(n log n)... For 12 queries on 200 contracts, the construction overhead exceeds the brute-force cost.'

expectations.text: Different paradigms are selected for different functions — not everything is solved with Space-Time Tradeoff
expectations.evidence: Report identifies 4 distinct paradigms: (1) Greedy for aplicar_pago_parcial, (2) Space-Time Tradeoff for rentabilidad_por_propiedad_mes, (3) 'None needed' for mejor_mes_mantenimiento, (4) Dynamic Programming (prefix sum) for cascada_morosidad. Explicitly explains why each paradigm fits and why alternatives don't.

expectations.text: validar_solapamiento_contratos is identified as NOT needing optimization due to small n (3-8 contracts per propiedad)
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'O(n²) on n=8 means at most 28 comparisons — trivially fast... Premature optimization here would reduce readability for zero measurable gain.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: generar_rol_cobros is identified as already efficient (O(n) with HashMap pre-indexing) and left unchanged
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'Total complexity: O(n + m) where n = contracts, m = properties. This is already optimal... The HashMap ensures no redundant scanning.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: propiedades_completamente_ocupadas is identified as already optimal and left unchanged
expectations.evidence: Report states: '.all() short-circuits on the first non-occupied unit, so average case is much better. A pre-indexed approach would add allocation overhead and complexity for no measurable benefit.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: contratos_por_vencer is identified as already efficient O(n) and the BTreeMap suggestion is rejected as over-engineering
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'A BTreeMap indexed by fecha_fin would... only pay off if you query the same dataset many times without rebuilding. Since this is called once daily, you'd rebuild every time anyway... The suggestion to use a BTreeMap is a classic case of over-engineering for small datasets.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: ingreso_mensual_por_moneda is identified as already optimal and left unchanged
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'Single O(n) pass over ~200 contracts, accumulating into a HashMap with at most 2 entries (DOP and USD)... textbook optimal.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: The output does NOT blindly optimize every function — at least 3 functions are explicitly left unchanged with justification
expectations.evidence: All 5 functions are explicitly left unchanged. Report concludes: 'No changes warranted. All five functions are appropriate for their dataset sizes and access patterns.'

expectations.text: reporte_envejecimiento's BTreeMap is preserved (not replaced with HashMap) because sorted output order is required
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'BTreeMap is intentional here: only 4 buckets, and sorted output is required for the report. No optimization needed.' Code preserves BTreeMap.

expectations.text: inquilinos_sin_atrasos is refactored to use HashMap/HashSet lookups eliminating the triple nested loop
expectations.evidence: Code builds HashSet<Uuid> of contratos_con_atraso and HashMap<Uuid, Vec<Uuid>> of contratos_por_inquilino. Report confirms: 'Optimized from O(inquilinos × contratos × pagos) to O(inquilinos + contratos + pagos) by pre-building lookup indexes.'

expectations.text: tendencia_pagos_acumulados is fixed with a cumulative/running total approach (not just HashMap grouping) to handle the prefix sum correctly
expectations.evidence: Code groups into HashMap, sorts months, then computes running sum: 'let mut acumulado = 0.0; meses.into_iter().map(|mes| { acumulado += totales_por_mes[&mes]; (mes, acumulado) })'. Report confirms: 'Single pass to compute running cumulative sum.'

expectations.text: detectar_pagos_duplicados is refactored to use a composite key (contrato_id + fecha_vencimiento + monto) for grouping instead of O(n²) pairwise comparison
expectations.evidence: Code uses: 'let key = (pago.contrato_id, pago.fecha_vencimiento, pago.monto.to_bits())' as HashMap key. Report confirms: 'Optimized from O(n²) to O(n) by grouping payments into buckets keyed by (contrato_id, fecha_vencimiento, monto_bits).'

expectations.text: resumen_metodos_pago is identified as already efficient and its BTreeMap is preserved for chronological ordering
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'BTreeMap is intentional here: months must be in sorted order for the chart. Single-pass aggregation is already optimal — no changes needed.' Code preserves BTreeMap.

expectations.text: The output explains WHY each BTreeMap is kept or replaced, not just mechanically swapping all BTreeMaps for HashMaps
expectations.evidence: Report explains for reporte_envejecimiento: 'BTreeMap is intentional here: only 4 buckets, and sorted output is required' and for resumen_metodos_pago: 'months must be in sorted order for the chart'. Each BTreeMap decision has explicit reasoning.

expectations.text: aplicar_pago_parcial is identified as NOT needing optimization (n=1-12 is too small, sort is appropriate for the greedy allocation)
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'With n=1-12 items, O(n log n) sort + O(n) greedy scan is optimal. A min-heap would be O(n) to build but O(n log n) to extract all elements, and we need to process ALL items in order.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: rentabilidad_por_propiedad_mes is refactored with multi-level pre-indexing (contrato-to-propiedad mapping AND pagos grouped by contrato AND gastos grouped by propiedad+month)
expectations.evidence: Code builds: (1) contrato_to_propiedad HashMap, (2) ingresos_index keyed by (Uuid, String), (3) egresos_index keyed by (Uuid, String). Report confirms: 'Pre-index with HashMaps... Build a contrato_id → propiedad_id lookup map, Group pagos by (propiedad_id, month), Group gastos by (propiedad_id, month).'

expectations.text: mejor_mes_mantenimiento is identified as already O(n) (12 is a constant) and left unchanged or noted as not needing optimization
expectations.evidence: Report states: 'The outer loop is fixed at 12 iterations. The inner scan is O(n) over ~200 contracts. Total work: 12 × 200 = 2400 comparisons. An interval tree or sweep line would add O(n log n) construction overhead.' Code preserved unchanged.

expectations.text: cascada_morosidad is fixed using a prefix sum or running total approach (group by month first, then accumulate) rather than just HashMap grouping
expectations.evidence: Code groups delinquent counts into BTreeMap per month, then computes prefix sum: 'let mut acumulado: usize = 0; for mes in &meses { resultado.push((mes.clone(), acumulado)); acumulado += impagos_por_mes.get(mes)... }'. Report identifies this as 'Decrease and Conquer (prefix sum)'.

expectations.text: The output correctly rejects at least one 'clever' suggestion (min-heap, interval tree) as over-engineering with clear reasoning about why
expectations.evidence: Report rejects both: (1) min-heap: 'A min-heap would be O(n) to build but O(n log n) to extract all elements... no advantage', (2) interval tree: 'An interval tree or sweep line would add O(n log n) construction overhead for a problem that's already O(n) with a tiny constant factor.' Clear reasoning provided.

expectations.text: Different paradigms are selected for different functions — not everything is solved with Space-Time Tradeoff
expectations.evidence: Report identifies distinct paradigms: (1) Greedy for aplicar_pago_parcial, (2) Transform and Conquer for rentabilidad_por_propiedad_mes, (3) Brute Force (keep) for mejor_mes_mantenimiento, (4) Decrease and Conquer (prefix sum) for cascada_morosidad. Summary table shows 4 different paradigm labels.
